data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × this child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the OWNER of parent AC3 ("The dual outbound applier paths are collapsed to one (the production path); the retired path is deleted") and parent SC2 ("Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path"). Both FULLY covered:
- 33d0 AC1 → exactly one outbound applier path remains in production; retired path deleted (no dead, separately-tested applier) → covers AC3 fully.
- 33d0 AC2 → single test drives fields+comments+labels+links+parent through the production path in one UPDATE, asserting each sub-op took effect → covers SC2 (production path exercised) + reinforces no-capability-drift.
- 33d0 AC3 → no capability regresses; full reconciler suite green → contributes to AC7.
- 33d0 AC4 → combined test asserts effects, not interaction/mock-call-counts → applies anti-change-detector posture to its own tests (contributes SC5/AC7).

Criteria this child does not own are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC1/AC2/SC1 → 8937 (schema SSOT + round-trip contract) + fe57 (fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake honesty); AC4/SC3 → 2359 (sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary); AC5/SC4 → 822a (hermetic bidirectional round-trip + de-encode 'expected-bug' guards). This child's declared OUT item (sub-op telemetry → story E/2359) is consistent with the roster. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 33d0 itself.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC3 + SC2 FULLY covered by this child; SC5/AC7 contributed; AC1/AC2/AC4/AC5/SC1/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below).

One ROSTER-LEVEL absence (not this child's fault; reportable per G3 — already raised on siblings 59ef/822a/8937/2359, restated for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the WHOLE roster including this child):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES the documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 33d0 AC4 "The combined test asserts effects (sub-op outcomes), not interaction/mock-call-counts" is self-scoped to THIS story's own test; 2359 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail governing new/future tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone) or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child 33d0 itself for its owned criteria (AC3/SC2 fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked on sibling tickets.
